message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily due to the absorption and scattering of sunlight by water. When sunlight enters the water, it is made up of different colors, each with varying wavelengths. Water absorbs colors at the red end of the spectrum more efficiently, while blue light, which has a shorter wavelength, is scattered and reflected back to our eyes. Additionally, factors such as the presence of phytoplankton, sediments, and the ocean's depth can influence the specific shade of blue observed. This interplay of light
